headline: Customs scandal: 47 containers of sanctioned goods cleared without inspection
context: Forty-seven containers of goods subject to international trade sanctions were cleared through Port Klang without mandatory inspection in 2025, exposing systemic customs enforcement failure.

cards.big: 47 containers of sanctioned goods cleared without inspection at Port Klang
cards.sub: Systemic customs failure puts Malaysia at risk of international trade sanctions

cards.big: Strategic Trade Act 2010 mandates inspection of all controlled goods shipments
cards.sub: 47 violations of mandatory inspection in a single port in one year

cards.big: Sanctioned goods transshipment threatens Malaysia's access to US and EU banking systems
cards.sub: Compliance failure risks trade sanctions that could cost billions in legitimate commerce

cards.big: Port Klang customs had 12 officers covering 3 shifts for 800 daily container movements
cards.sub: 4 officers per shift for 800 containers makes inspection physically impossible

cards.big: Malaysia isn't choosing to violate sanctions — it simply doesn't have the capacity to comply
Should customs enforcement be dramatically expanded before Malaysia faces international consequences?
